analysis.name: Abigail Drake
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 39 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This professor is very sarcastic. She makes the lecture environment very uncomfortable with shady remarks towards people's questions, for example. To put it quite bluntly, she's rude to students. She acts like she has a surperiority complex about her teaching style. I am sure many of the reviews would agree with this.

most of her bad reviews are from the class itself. Abby's a pretty nice prof, and offers to go over missed problems from the prelim + lots of extra cred. that said, BIOEE1780 has an excessive amount of work. lots of memorization, field trips, and pre-lecture assignments. tip: vodcasts and lectures are a MUST, but dont waste your time on the reading

She seems convinced her teaching method is inherently superior and beyond criticism. Team-Based Learning is treated more like a belief system than a tool, despite working poorly for many students. The focus feels ego-driven rather than student-centered, with too much time wasted on forced group work.

I wrote a bad review of Prof. Drake, and felt bad so had it removed. Retrospectively, I wish I kept it up. She is the single worst professor I've had at Cornell who will literally laugh in your face and make you feel small. The subject matter of her course was interesting, but the course structure itself was painstakingly boring. I warn everyone!!

Honestly the rudest professor I've had so far at cornell . Pretends like she wants her students to laugh and foster and comfortable environment for learning but does the opposite. Would not take a class with her again.

analysis.name: Andrei  Guruianu
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This FWS is 2x the work of others, but you emerge with a very solid grasp on expository writing and maybe even a new worldview. You also get helpful advice from your TA--most FWS's lack one. Dr. G himself is an acquired taste. To some, he is refreshingly blunt; to others, he seems self-important. To the former camp (me), he's pretty fun to talk to.

This class gives you what you put in.. or so I thought. Take this class if you need to fulfill FWS and don't mind getting a high B or low A. Professor is caring and the class lectures are extremely surface-level. The real education is when you do the readings and write on your own. I recommend just reading John Berger's "Ways of Seeing" instead.

Awesome Professor! His class improved my writing significantly. He was especially helpful in individual meetings and always gave insightful feedback on my work. I believe, however, that this class grades harsher relative to other FWS. If you want an easy A, this is not the class for you. If you want to improve your writing, then I highly recommend.

Fun and Engaging! Great Professor if your main goal is to improve your writing capabilities, Otherwise it isn't worth it to take the class as a freshman, grading is comparatively harsh compared to other offerings, with courses offering B's just by showing up to class, choose those if you are just trying to fulfill your FWS requirement.

analysis.name: Alan Nussbaum
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Mostly lectures and tended to go over the time of the class by like 10 minutes consistently. Somewhat easy but lowkey didn't complete the amount we were supposed to finish the semester so the next semester we had to do more to make up for it.

Great professor. Really laid back, doesn't give many homework assignments or tests. Essentially, he'll teach the amount you're willing to learn. We read Ovid--about as interesting as Nussbaum himself, but it's not like classes are dreadfully boring.Very nice, funny man. Looks like he's headed towards retirement, take a class of his while you can.

A very enjoyable professor, very knowledgeable and open to questions. All material is on printed notes handed out each class, but not much of it will make sense if you don't go to lecture. I did have his class in the late afternoon and his voice put me to sleep several times, but not for lack of interesting content.

analysis.name: Anton Mosunov
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Very cool lecturer, has GOAT potential imo, but for a class called "Linear Algebra for Engineers", he really expects you to learn the material as if you were an upcoming mathmatician in the 1600s. Extremely proof heavy, prelims containted T/F sections but also sections where you prove theorems you learned in class.

Mosunov actively wants you to succeed. His lectures are great and the iclicker questions are actually really helpful for learning (shocking I know). The hw was easy altho there were optional problem sets that better prepared you for the exams and quizzes but they aren't necessary. Pay attention early and you will be rewarded.
Nice guy and cares about his students but made the class a million times more difficult than it had to be

One of the most wonderful and caring professors I've had at Cornell. His lecture slides were amazing for learning all of the content necessary. The class was very hard because of the lack of a curve and very proof-heavy exams, but completely doable if you put in the effort to do the problem sets.

Lowkey a goated prof, cares about students, explains concepts very well, passionate. Exams were all fair, basically no HW, u needed to just study yourself. Exams includes proofs, some were chill, some were harder.

analysis.name: Aditya Vashistha
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: An excellent exploration of global perspectives in computing. Challenges technological saviorism attitudes. Prof. Vashistha is incredibly engaging, passionate, and knowledgeable. There is a significant group project due at the end and reading quizzes before classes, but it was a reasonable class with reasonable expectations. Very worthwhile.
This was one of the best courses I have attended. The course opened up completely new perspectives on me and the learning will help me regardless of what I do next.

Great professor, he is very passionate and knowledgeable about the subject. He clearly cares about the content and has a great, engaging lecture style that makes it easy to be interested in the subject. Reading heavy and attendance required, though.

Professor Vashistha is nice and really cares about the content of this class. There's a lot of reading but nothing that's too much to handle. The only hard part of this class is the final project where you have to start super early and show that you put a lot of thought and work into it to do well. Other than the final project, super manageable.
One of the best course that I took at Cornell. Great way to learn how to design and build technology for underserved communities in different parts of the world. Strongly recommended.

analysis.name: Audrey  Baker
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: If there was a tag for differentiation, I'd add that. She spreads negativity against certain people in her weird course.
Is she mentally stable? Maybe she needs some help. This program needs to conduct a health check-up for their faculty.

What's the ATTITUDE about? Has a major support from the program but lacks respect. She assumes she is EXCLUSIVE and has been mean to students this year. She told me you will be graduating with lesser skills. So basically she is proving that she is NOT a great instructor.
I felt uncomfortable during office hours due to rude comments. I also did not receive support with internship recommendations, which was disappointing.
The course description is different compares to what actually is taught in the course. A lot of discrepancy exists and this course needs to be STOPPED for God's Sakes!!

analysis.name: Anne Bracy
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 75 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Little to no TAs use the piazza, and some of the answers are rather rude. Tests have means in the 50s and are unrelated to anything learned. Lectures have minimal overlap with projects, and the projects are very hazily laid out. For one project, we were told to simply google any questions. It's a shame this course is mandatory.

I don't know whether my negative experience with prof bracy owes itself more to the overbearing nature of the class (and I mean this class approaches levels of work akin to 19th century industrial revolution standards of labor), or to her stone cold, stoic conveyance of the material. Bracy was relentless and didn't even try to help make it palatabl

The class is harder than it has to be. Concepts in lecture are briefly touched upon, with the majority of content being self-taught. Lectures and projects have little overlap, and tests are hard to predict. Bracy is good at teaching in lecture, but little of what she teaches is actually useful for the tests and projects.
Awesome teaching with low scores. Think thoroughly before you enroll!!

Overall, I wish there was more thought put into the course. Projects were not well planned, had too much time to complete, but little support. Having weekly problem sets would have helped students know what to focus on. Many difficult topics on the exams were covered in the book, but glossed on in lecture. Had to self-study to do well on exams.

analysis.name: Happiness Bulugu
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ms. Happiness is genuinely one of the greatest teachers that i have ever had. She is very understanding and cares very much about building a community with the students of her classes, makes class feel like a family.

Bi Happiness is one of the best professors out there! She is so nice and caring! I took this class as a language requirement, but it ended up being my favorite class of the semester. For learning a new language, Bi Happiness makes it super enjoyable and easy. Weekly assignments help you improve both speaking and writing. 10/10 would recommend

I love Happiness so much. I've learned a lot of Swahili from her and would encourage anyone to take her class. If you want to skim by and get an A, it's totally doable. If you really wanted to learn Swahili that's doable too. What you put into this course is what you get out of it. I love starting my day with Happiness and my Swahili class

Happiness is the best language professor/teacher I have ever had. She is unbelievably caring and genuinely interested in her students. Swahili is a fun language to learn and Happiness creates a low-stress environment to do so. I look forward to her class every day and no Friday classes are a perk :) I highly, highly recommend!!

Bulugu is, simply put, a great professor. Swahili with her is always fun and is an easy course so long as you put in just a little time going over vocab/grammar each week. Because of the small class size, you'll definitely bond with your classmates. Only good things to say.

analysis.name: Nicolas Buchon
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 45 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He's amazing! If you put your all into his classes you will be rewarded. Yes it will be the hardest class of your life but you will not regret a single moment- be sure to take advantage of office hours and ask questions!
I took that class as an external student. The content was very interesting, and challenging. I really appreciated the feedback from the Professor, who answered all my questions quickly.

He's really straightforward and funny in lectures, makes it very engaging. He also had more in-lecture polling questions than Dr.Helmann and Dr.Pesciotta, and that helped me understand and engage with the topic more. Generally a really fun bio class, especially if you have a bit of background (e.g. IB HL Biology). Loved his martial art shirts.

I thought he was a pretty good prof, but his lectures were the most difficult for me to understand out of the 3 profs. His were so extremely content-dense that it was nearly impossible to actually memorize everything. Though the material was extremely interesting, and I thoroughly enjoyed learning it all, even though it took a lot of work to do so.
The lectures are amazing. I consider changing major and taking more biology because of this class.

analysis.name: Lance Bush
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Do not take this class if you don't like scientific writing. All he grades you on is the accuracy of your thoughts and not your writing. Class is also boring and he overcomplicates ideas for no reason. Grade is made up of 5 essays with no homework but you have 20+ page readings before class. If you want a fun and easy FWS, this is not for you.

Professor Bush is deeply committed to ensuring his students enjoy the learning process. He is very accommodating and often shares stories about his home life and family, which shows that he genuinely values building meaningful connections with his students. While the material can be complex at times, just continue to ask questions and participate.

Take his FWS. Interesting, sometimes confusing concepts, but he cares a lot about his students and will review drafts and devote many hours to helping his students. Compared to other FWS, this class is not a lot of work.

One of my favorite professors at Cornell. Super super chill and, especially for an FWS, there is not a lot of work and there is no attendance. Four three-page papers throughout the semester and then quite a long paper at the end but he guides you through most of the writing. Wants students to succeed and it super into the class he teaches.
Great professor. Some of the topics can be confusing at times. He is super helpful outside of class. The only thing is that the subject was too philosophical for me.

analysis.name: Elizabeth Fisher
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Clearly has passion for propulsion, very kind person and intelligent also

One of my favorite lecturers at Cornell. Clearly very passionate about her work and her students. Takes time to thoroughly address questions during lecture. Her teaching style is extremely engaging and she makes the material fun to learn!
Fisher is the goat, love her. I think I may become a professional thermophysicist or thermomatician or whatever you call it because of her. Shout out to Sadi Carnot
She is a great lecturer!! I am not a fan of thermodynamics, but she makes it way easier to understand and talks her way slowly through material that makes it easier to understand.

Hands down best professor I've had at Cornell. Didn't know what a meaningful lecture was until I had her as a lecturer. I only used the textbook as a supplement to study, but it wasn't really necessary. VERY fair exams, not easy, just fair given what we cover in class and on the HW. Actually cares about students & our wellbeing!

analysis.name: Siddhartha Banerjee
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This whole course is an unorganized messy crap. The most unresponsive prof ever in my life ....... don't think anyone can beat him on this.

Very bad teaching skills. Unorganized lectures. Most of the time, he got confused by his own questions. Expect a lot of homework assignments. Nothing useful/substantial taught in second half of the semester. I would recommend taking ORIE 5581 if you still want to take this course unless you want to earn extra 2 credits without any efforts.

The class is pretty disorganized 😅. We have one more week of classes: still need Prelim 1 back from October😅, the final date is a mystery😅, project is due same day as final.. don't 
Know what project entails😅.Lecture content jumps around😅, some (quite a few) questions on Ed go unanswered😅. Honestly, I am scared😅

He's REALLY disorganized. We have not gotten our prelim 1 back from October. We don't know the date of the final (there's only a week left of class), our project is supposedly due the day of the final (we know nothing about the project), there has not been a single lecture where he goes in order. He avoids questions on ed he cannot answer. vibes 😃

It is currently November 24th... here is the fact pattern:

We have had 6 HWs due throughout the semester. 5 have been graded
1 Exam on 10/16, have not gotten it back
A final he said was 12/2, then said 12/4 and is now unscheduled for next week, with no practice 
A final project with no details, no due date
A final HW with no details, no due date

analysis.name: Brett Fors
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Fors is AMAZING!! Best chemistry professor I've ever had. He truly cares so much about his students and really makes an effort to ensure we understand. He is very organized, and his lectures are easy to follow

Although Fors just started teaching, nearly everyone loved him. His tests are fair, and the materials come directly from the notes. As long as you study, you will get what you deserve. However, he goes through a lot in each lecture so prepare to study ahead of time. He is a gem and will make organic chem actually enjoyable.

Professor Fors is an OUTSTANDING professor and the best professor I have had so far at Cornell. His lectures are so easy to follow and incredibly engaging. I love his side notes at the end of class as well. He is also very accessible outside of class, and it is obvious that he genuinely cares about his students' learning.
Fors is genuinely one of the nicest professors I've had here at Cornell. His lectures are super accessible and easy to follow and he really cares about the questions that students have!

the NICEST and most approachable professor. v passionate about orgo and is reflected by his teaching. he truly wants his students to succeed and cares about them a lot. he is the only reason why i went to orgo. he tries to make orgo fun by giving us his silly fun fact of the orgo every class. sign me up for his fan club. he a smiley guy for sure.

analysis.name: Ewa Bachminska
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Does not "believe" in giving 100s. Soft spoken, but speaks audaciously to children, students, and all. Very strict classroom rules. No leeway for attendance. Thought the class content was okay and easy, but disliked it purely because of her.

The class was easy content wise. She has a bunch of nonsense rules on classroom behavior. If you enter the classroom before class time with earbuds in you will be reprimanded. You are not allowed to use the bathroom during class either, does not realize that we are adults. She is also very confrontational.
The class is easy but the professor will make you regret it. You've got to be on her favorites list to get by easily.

Very poor instructions on assignments followed by grading on criteria that was never stated. English is not her first language and it clearly shows when your paper is read. Forces you to limit your writing. Its not too hard to get an A if you try and guess what she wants, but it could get frustrating.

I came into class not knowing a single word of Polish and had a terrific experience. Ewa is super fair and thoughtful on grading, and is extremely supportive. There are straightforward rules in the class... you need to be willing to follow and respect them. Polish is a unique course that I highly recommend versus more traditional language courses.

analysis.name: Oona Cullen
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the few classes I've felt fully immersed into when going to class. Participation is a large part of class which became very productive over time helping me learn how to look at material differently. Grading based on criteria and improving, so it becomes a class you can enjoy material for instead of stressing your grade. Great teacher!
She is very nice, who encourages you to answer questions and participate in class discussions. The class atmosphere is chill and respectful. She grades you based on completion.
She's really nice and grades based on personal improvement and completion. Classes include a lot of close reading, working with peers, and discussion.

analysis.name: Laura Staum Casasanto
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: COGST1101 was a mixed bag. There were times when it was very interesting and thought-provoking, and other times when there were no thoughts to be had. The format of the class is not great, as most of the time, students are giving presentations on the content and not Casasanto. If she taught more, I think her class would be more interesting.

This was one of my favorite classes. A good amount of reading but no homework, I enjoyed her lectures. I didn't like having to listen to students' presentations because most were not very good. Her tests were so easy, two short-answer exams (mostly had to argue well) and 1 MCQ. You can do a group presentation in place of an exam. Easiest A+ for me.

Professor was okay, but did not actually teach most of the class. She would only give 1 of the 5 presentations each class, with a majority of time spent listening to other students' group presentations. Her lectures were solid and covered the reading well, but beware of the class format. Exams were a bit tough, but doable if you did the readings.

She's a great professor. This class is all group projects and Socratic seminar. If you like talking about current events and are very opinionated, you'll love it. DO NOT take this class if you have a fear of public speaking. This is the kind of class where 1 super liberal & 1 super conservative kid fight the whole time. Lot of fun.

analysis.name: Julieta Caunedo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She is not a great lecturer. She goes really fast during lectures and jumps from topic to topic so be ready to take notes and lock in the whole time, or just self study. She also has an accent which can be hard to pick up sometimes. The workload was alright with group problem sets and mostly just exams.

Enraged by Econ 3040. Lectures are unclear, and grading is arbitrary and inconsistent between exams. Content unclear throughout. The stats are concerning, but prof said it's normal. Unresponsive before final. Got sick (102° fever), but makeup would mean "I" on the transcript. Never been so frustrated with a course.

Professor Caunedo disregarded syllabus grading and gave much lower grades for many students than was outlined in the provided syllabus. The lectures were difficult to follow, yet the content was easy to digest once reviewed outside of class. Highly recommend taking a different professor for 3040 if possible
CURVED DOWN EVERYONE'S GRADES BY A HALF-LETTER. Does not follow her own grading rubric. Reads directly off of slides in thick accent. Homeworks not related to lecture material. Do not recommend.

I have learnt a ton for this course. The professor was available to answer questions outside class and always clear when asked for clarification. My letter grade was wrong and was freaking out because of internship applications but she fixed it by the morning after.

analysis.name: David Collum
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher, but doesn't like it when you yawn. He has a pleasant side and a dark side. Proceed with caution especially if you have to yawn during a lecture
This guy is a literal Nazi who thinks Hitler was great and the Holocaust was mostly made up. Puts his Nazi attitudes and racist comments in his class repeatedly.
David seems to know at least a little of chemistry, but spends most of the class arguing that the Holocaust was the fault of America and the Soviet Union.
He was professional and funny making difficult scientific analogies to simple life lessons. It made material more understandable.
Amazing Prof. Most honest and knowledgeable Prof or person I've ever known. If your willing to learn and have an open mind, no one will teach you more. Wish he was my Prof for every class.

analysis.name: Christina Homrighouse
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 51 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I am so thankful that I had a Christina as a teacher for EDA. I now graduated and she is even willing to help with a excel-based work assignment that I have been struggling with. She genuinely cares about her students and this class is the most useful class that I have taken throughout my time at Cornell.

Useful class, but can be rude and aggressive. This instructor is retiring anyway, but just putting it out there as a warning. Def agrees with the comments earlier down here that she can be condescending and unreasonable.

Christina is literally the best and most genuine person on campus. She is so invested in each student and she develops their technical, professional, and personal skills. The topics she teaches are taught with ingenuity and are the most practical and important skills any student can ask for. She is a FANTASTIC educator and friend. Thank you Mrs. H!

Christina is a phenomenal teacher. She knows her stuff and she is willing to work with students who may be struggling. Christina cares about her students and wants them all to succeed if they put in the required amount of effort. I recommend going to every class and taking notes. Excel will help you not only at Cornell but well afterwards.

Easy A+ class.  Cant really skip class that often, but Homrighouse tells you exactly what you have to do on the projects and what will be on the quizzes.  GO TO OFFICE HOURS, she shows you how to do everything so that you really learn it.  The material is easy. Take this class!

analysis.name: Kevin Clermont
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Clermont is a brilliant scholar but unable to make the material understandable for his students.  Lectures go off on irrelevant tangents, leaving less time for important material.  The exam was absolutely ridiculous and does not measure how well the students learned the semester's material.  Sorry Cornell 1Ls, you're stuck with him one semester.

I aced Clermont's class but the one thing I'll note is that his class is very lecture focused. Random iClicker questions, previous practice problems, and off-the-cuff lecture tangents morph into exam questions so you sort of need to write down everything he says. Being old school he's also purposefully opaque. And the textbook? LMAO.

Clermont makes it near impossible to learn civil procedure, one of the hardest first year classes.  He doesn't realize his students haven't been studying it for 50 years like he has.  But too bad.  All 1Ls have to take him.

Teaches to the top 10% of the class, and the rest of his students are left scratching their heads. Prides himself on the fact that he gives out incredibly difficult finals.  Unfortunately, every 1L can expect to have him.

The best professor I've had so far. Prepare for class and think about the material and you will get a lot out of this. So far he is the only teacher to write multiple choice questions that don't have mistakes in them that make the answers ambiguous. People see issues and know the material will do well in this class, and people who don't won't.

analysis.name: Celina Nateram
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Nateram was in a roundtable and development chat, and she went the extra mile to show everyone principles of a good contract and also talked about cybersecurity how important it is to review data we put into AI models and how we store information. She is so sweet and smart, if you get a chance to take her class, do it!

It's an ivy league school, so expect it to be challenging and difficult. Not sure about the other ratings but i learned alot from her and the course. I really like cornell's professional development classes and all the professors they have. Celina always took the time to have a coffee chat with students after going through the materials. 10/10

Celina was an OUTSTANDING instructor for the Power and Ethics in Negotiation class.Her energy, intelligence and teaching style made the live sessions both fun and informative. She provided meticulous feedback, showing genuine care for students' growth.Cornell's cert programs are pricey but worth the investment, I'd love to take more of her courses.

I really loved her instruction on my courses at Cornell and despite my challenges between being a busy mom and having a career, she made the time to respond to all my questions and genuinely felt she cared about the well being of students. I love Cornell and having professors like her.

analysis.name: Cole Gilbert
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 45 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This professor is boring and disorganized. I have not gotten lower than an "A" until this 1440 Biology class. If you think looking at slides all course and cracking silly jokes is fun great. Exams had no continuity with lectures! Should have gotten out of his course. Then half the final grades posted were wrong and the final grades reposted - WOW!

He didn't get everyone falling asleep in class, but that DOESN'T MEAN he was a good professor. Lecture content was VERY different from test material, and the class WAS NOT curved. The average was supposed to be a C, but he widened the bins so everyone got a B-. Then the very last day of grading, he changed half the grades due to math error. RUN.

very kind and funny, flexible about deadlines, and reasonable grading.

That said, this class is extremely hard if you don't already have a very strong understanding of insect bio it will take up a lot of time to memorize and learn everything.
Very interesting lecturer and class. Exams tended to be specific, but graded fairly. Lab portion is a lot of work with making the collection

Dr. Gilbert is not a good professor...he flies through his powerpoints without taking a breath...it's impossible to catch hardly any of what he is saying...his tests are unbearably difficult b/c of the large amount of material he inadequately covers..if you want to pass his class, be prepared to put in lot of extra work teaching yourself everything

analysis.name: Christian Danescu
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
Ridiculous class policies, he doesn't allow you to use pencils on prelims or write on the actual exam. 

When asked why he said, "If you are looking into a teaching career and want to find out more about the motivation behind each of the decisions, please come to my OH and I'll be happy to share my experience with you."

The orginzation in this class is extremely poor. No notes, answer keys, practice answers are posted so it doesn't feel encouraging to learn anyting someone might've not understood the first time. The actual content should be very sraightforward and simple, but due to the organization it makes everything unecessarily confusing and laborious.

Terrible experience, avoid at all costs. Assignments are tedious and do not contribute to learning the material itself. Lectures didn't make any sense because of his completely incomprehensible accent. I had to self-study the entire course because lectures were all useless. Please hire a new prof to teach this class.

One of the best CS profs I've had, really fun class where you actually use what you learn at on the final project, the assignments and the midterm are pretty chill, attendance does matter, prof teaches the material pretty well for everyone of all backgrounds (no ML or ML), and is really passionate about IR system designs and the Kardashians. 10/10.

DNM is super funny and is a great professor. The class gives lots of opportunities to gain extra credit, and the grading for everything is very lenient. You will learn a lot of applicable information retrieval techniques, and although the class might have some questionable/annoying policies, overall it is worthwhile and fun thanks to DNM!

analysis.name: Kelly Delp
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Kelly Delp is clearly very passionate about math, but one of the issues I had is that she would focus lots of time on the conceptual aspect of problems whereas Math 1920 is more of an application class rather than a proof class. Her notes are very helpful and posts skeleton and final notes after lectures. She's great but do more practice problems.

Focused a lot more on the general idea of the math and why it was cool rather than how to actually do it. We were told why something was important before how it worked. Because of this, I ended up learning mostly from posted notes and the textbook. You could tell she was passionate about the math though, and that was not always contagious.
She is very energetic during lectures and is open to answering questions. Uses a lot of visual representations, which helps with understanding the 3D structures in multi.

Great teacher--you get out what you put in, and I definitely put in mid levels of effort for an okay grade. Awesome lecturer and a total math nerd. I felt like some parts of the class were taught in a very unintuitive way that just didn't mesh with my learning style, but the assigned textbook generally explains things well.

Delp is great! The policies for her course are very clear and straightforward, and she provides skeleton notes before every class so people know what we are covering. The lectures aren't my favorite environment, and I often relied on the textbook to supplement them, but that's ultimately a skill issue: Delp teaches incredibly well.

analysis.name: Robert DiStasio
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 38 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: You'd think this man has a pizza place in Brooklyn, not a PhD from Berkeley in Quantum Mechanics. Crazy hard tests, but he made them shorter so that he can get out of campus earlier to his hot date (aka wife). Take this class, you will not be disappointed (except with your grade).

Truly the greatest of his kind. Robby was a solid 5 stars through and through. Genuinely cared for the students. He speaks a bit like a cartoon character but you get used to it. 
-signed Robby's #1 fan

A little funny but he clearly doesn't want to teach. He's definitely not a terrible person but I found him very inaccessible towards the end of the semester which was frankly aggravating. 

Lectures were just textbook but he sometimes helped clarify some details. Maybe I should've just skipped class and watched youtube videos on the subject.

He is a pretty good lecturer but his exams were the complete opposite of the practice problems he provided. the previous year's exams that he gave were 10x easier than our exams and the material taught during lecture was not reflective of exam materials at all. he is very knowledgeable of the material and kind, but the exams were way too difficult.

He's a great guy who cares about his students and makes the time to help people, but his tests are pretty difficult and does not provide enough materials to prepare for those caliber of questions. Lectures are straight from the textbook and I would recommend reading the textbook, especially parts you're having trouble with. Great dude, mid teacher.

analysis.name: Peter Diamessis
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Very good chalkboard lectures for class, very dry content. Worst part of class is group homework and group final project -- if you get a bad group / group member that just does not want to meet, its hard to learn the content. Learn by working with friends, group with them if possible. He does not know Python, but has great theoretical knowledge.
Prof. Diamessis is great. No matter how challenging or coding‑heavy the class can feel at the time, the ideas will only become more relevant. Definitely go to class and participate!

Very funny guy. Gives off the energy of the basketball coach who's forced to teach a class, except he's actually a decent lecturer and not actually a basketball coach. The psets are pretty long and require a solid background in coding. Idk why last years class resented him so much. People generally seemed to like him and the class this year

diamessis is a great guy once you get to talk to him, this class can be difficult and I understand why he might get a bad rep, but if you put even an ounce of effort to talking to him about needing help with the class he will help you!

He chooses his course time for a slot that is honestly waay too early, you'll notice that fewer students show up as a result. Problem set every week but assigned for teams of two, which makes things a little easier. Nice guy, though sometimes a little jumbled. Receives and utilizes student feedback effectively.

analysis.name: Ricardo Daziano
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: TAs make or break this class. Content is very dry. Professor does a lot of examples during class which are worth doing in the long run, as they give you extra practice. They are also what is tested. Recommend learning LaTeX notation for this class. Assignments were a pain and not helpful usually, but ZyBook HW was. Final was ridiculously long.

Lectures were dry and heavy on theoretical information. Recitations were somewhat helpful but the many assignments were extremely difficult and required asking questions or stumbling through searching for specific coding functions. The class felt like it was preparing students for a PhD rather than just a masters level course. High expecations.

Daziano is a good lecturer, but outside of lecture the course was really disorganized. The recitations felt useless, and the online homework portal was really frustrating. If you studied the slides and did the homework, it was fairly easy to do well enough. Also, some lectures were way too R/Stata/Minitab heavy to be engaging.

analysis.name: Daniel Szpiro
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Amazing professor. He is very passionate about the subject and will make sure that you understand everything needed for the tests. He wants to see you succeed. The grading structure of the class is designed for you to do well, with 25% of the class grading being based on online HW and quizzes that you should easily be able to get 100s on.

This man is the GOAT. Truly so understanding and kind and always willing to help you out. Also incredible at responding to emails. I had to take this class for my minor, and I am glad I took it with him. It is a difficult course, but he does his best to make sure you succeed. THE GOAT!

For such a boring subject, Professor Szpiro was an extremely kind and helpful professor. I had conflicts with every exam, and he worked tirelessly to help accommodate other students. Additionally, he has given some very helpful extra credit! Also, he starts every class with a joke, so that's fun.

Dr. Szpiro is very caring and understanding. That being said, the lectures honestly didn't help much for prelims. Just grind Mcgraw Hill and read the textbook. With so many cracked ppl in this class, the curve isn't lax but an A or A- aren't difficult to achieve. You're allowed a page of notes on tests, so the exam vibe is pretty chill.

The professor himself was an amazing person. This class was extremely difficult partly because of him and myself. THE TAS WERE VERY WEIRD THO. I once met with a Ta and he spent the time yelling at his girlfriend on the phone... Pay attention to lectures and the moment you get confused talk to him about it. He is very open and kind! lots of respect

analysis.name: Dan Kowal
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: Basically no curve. Which he said he would. Like my raw grade was a B+ and I end with a B+. IDK maybe I was not scoring meaningfully more to get a A-. But he is so unbelievably rude. Like his lectures are good and easy to follow but he is insanely rude. Like talking to him feels insulting at times and he grades really hard.

He was not communicative at all, rude and dismissive to those who wanted to talk about grades, and was not transparent about grading (no curve). TAs graded your assignments twice, often resulting in lower grades. Cheating on exams was rampant. If he teaches this again, don't take it. For the stats major, you are probably better off in ECON 3140.

Dr. Kowal was a nice prof, and I believe he made BTRY 4030 as interesting as he could. It's not a very applied course by nature, but he tried hard to give us homework and exam problems which were applied  to the real world, which I enjoyed. The work load in this class was manageable, but exams were hard in my opinion.

He was very dry and seemed pretty unapproachable and uninterested in the class. He was EXTREMELY unclear about grading policies (although i will recognize it was his first time teaching the course) and just overall vague and uncommunicative. The class is a major requirement but didn't even feel useful. Don't really recommend

Yes, he can be boring with dry lectures. BUT, incredibly approachable, very helpful when asking questions, gives few homeworks and they're easy, gives easy exams, and will often take your side when you have regrade requests. Exams are basically the same as practice exams and there are no trick questions.

analysis.name: Dmitry Savransky
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Savransky is an understanding and caring professor. He is lenient with grading at the end of the semester, allowing students to complete an extra credit problem set. With that said, it was difficult to retain much from lectures in the course, as a flipped-classroom approach was implemented. Homework assignments were also extremely long.

Most organized and clear class I have ever taken. Lectures are super interesting if you watch the videos ahead of time, like you are supposed to. He is a really funny guy who helps everyone make light of what is definitely a tough class. I think he lives on Ed and gives super great explanations to any questions. Had a really great experience

Really was great to learn from as someone who never did dynamics before - everything was super clear and if anyone had a question he usually responded in less than 20 minutes online. All lecture content was online in videos/handouts which made everything super easy to reference. Homework is hard, but lectures are super engaging and interesting

Horrible professor that makes a hard class harder. Tests are easier than hws but the homework's are excruciating. Extremely tough grader. ended with a 93.9 and that was an A-. Lectures were entirely unhelpful for hw or exams. primarily poll ev based. not understanding or nice
basically a self study class, why pay so much to go to university when you have to learn content on your own?

analysis.name: Elke Siegel
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Elke's lectures are quite fluid and free-form, but she is one of the most caring and genuine professors at this school. She encourages participation through engagement and creates an environment where students feel comfortable sharing their ideas. Do the readings — if you stay on track, you'll do great. I'd highly encourage ANY course of hers.

She was filling in for a colleague on sabbatical so IDK if she'll teach the course again. Made me fall in love with humanities. Obvious she cared about all her students in and out of class, was willing to talk about anything. Clear grader...and everyone got an A+

Elke Siegel is hands down my favorite professor at Cornell. I've taken three German classes with her and it is very apparent that she is there to teach you German - she doesn't give busy work and she doesn't grade unfairly. If anything she is a too nice a grader. If you have any interest in taking a GERST classs, take Siegel's!!!

I took Frau Siegel's class Lyric Poetry, which I never thought I would take because I hate poetry. But she made it super interesting and I would definitely recommend this course if she teaches it again! If you're into German whatsoever you absolutely have to take a class with her. And on top of that she's a really nice grader.

My hatred for poetry is long and enduring; I was in the class to improve my German. I agree with the previous reviewer, that anyone who speaks German should take a class with Professor Siegel.   If you only have time in your schedule to fit in one German class, take whatever class Siegel is offering. You won't regret it.

analysis.name: Irene Eibenstein-Alvisi
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

GREAT TEACHER!! So passionate and knows so much about Dante's inferno. This is not an easy class, but great for improving writing and analysis. is based off of effort/improvement and you can get an A (not based off of individual grades)

IEA very clearly cares a lot about her class and her role as a teacher. She is there to help you improve in your writing and will NOT give an easy A for FWS. She will give a lot of feedback and help you get better. If you put in the work and clearly try to improve, you'll get good grade and you'll learn a fair amount. Def more work than other FWS.

One of, if not the, best first-year writing seminar professors. Her feedback is incredibly detailed. Instead of simply giving out letter grades and due dates, she expects you to improve your essays as much as possible through office hours. All grades in her course are based on the final drafts at the end of the semester: she focuses on improvement!

I love this prof, the topics were so interesting and we read/watching things about love so most of our assignments were a response to them. The final project was ab a topic of your choosing, it was so helpful to have meetings with her ab the essays and she's super understanding. My new fws doesn't compare-also the community in the class was on top

killed my love for architecture

The class is well organized and structured for student success. Expectations and grading criteria are clearly outlined for assignments and exams. He makes really hard assignments especially the last few, which can take up to 15-20 hours. He also teaches certain topics in a very theoretical way. the sun of a beach. He is mentally challenged.
horrible professor. whats on the exam does not line up with what is taught in class. if you can take a different stats class do it. dropped it after the first exam because it was so horrible

Eshan's lectures were straightforward and his exams were difficult but fair. Much of the lecture material is covered in the textbook, but attending and participating in lecture helped to gain a deeper understanding of the concepts.

Can't explain intuitively, most students rely on AI to explain concepts. Topics are not useful (it's a theory class, so completely fine of course), but also not fun. 
Over-focused on NP reductions. Overall least favorite class I've taken at Cornell.
Not the best at explaining things. Algo is a tough class in general but can't say that having him as a prof made it better.

analysis.name: Emily Foster
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Her class was an absolute breeze work wise, and the content (I took No Spoilers!) was interesting. The workload never exceeds a fairly sized essay and she is genuinely one of the kindest people on earth. She's extremely lenient/understanding which was really helpful during tough times, & cares about her students. TAKE A CLASS WITH HER SHES AMAZING

Dr. Foster was excellent, and I would highly recommend taking any of her classes. All classwork and reading is super manageable and interesting. I feel as though my writing genuinely improved throughout the FWS. She gives great, specific feedback on all writing.

Great Prof for my FWS; super kind and understanding of people's other responsibilities outside of his class. Her feedback is really helpful, and the classwork is very light. Overall, great prof, and would definitely take her class again!

ENGL 3747 was a breath of fresh air! Grade is based on participation (attendance and speaking), 3 short book reports, mid-term, final essay, and a presentation. The assignments were manageable, fun, and included many options for us to choose from. Foster was charming and engaging, making the plethora of readings worthwhile! A must-take at Cornell.

Took ENGL3747. Dr. Foster is hands down the best professor at Cornell. This was my favorite class! It is not burdensome at all, but there is a lot of reading (super fun though) so don't fall behind. The class is pretty discussion heavy and you're technically graded on it. Highly recommend to anyone remotely interested in crime fiction in any form.

analysis.name: Frans Schalekamp
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: We spent half of every lecture on review, and though that boosted our test scores, it meant that there was no point in going to class. If you're solely here to pass the class, just read the course pack before tests. You'll thrive. However, you will miss his fantastic shirt collection and jolly attitude--he is genuinely a delightful person.

he's a genuinely passionate and kind professor who wants his students to succeed. this class made me switch my major to ORIE. he designed his course pack well and he's friendly and responsive on edstem + office hours. homework can be tricky but exams are fair. his high rating is so deserved. going to try to take his classes in the future

frans is a good lecturer but lecture did move slow at times. in general his lectures will give you a better understanding than just reading the textbook though. the class is structured very fairly and many resources such as discussion section worksheets were posted (for all sections) to use as practice before exams.

Frans is really chill, and the class is slow-paced. The first half of the semester covered materials less than expected, so he had to rush through all SVD, but overall Frans is a really good professor! Take 2940 with him if you can!

He's an amazing professor! The lectures are engaging and funny, and he explains everything in an intuitive way instead of making concepts complicated. The psets can take time but it's not too difficult to figure it out + it's fun once you get the hang of it. Tests were straightforward and on the easier side. This class made me consider orie.

Flavien is a good person deep down, but if you care about your GPA, getting an A is hard. I took french for 8 years now and with putting in the work, I struggled to maintain a good grade. He grades essays extremely hard (I recommend using ai), his tests are hard, and so are his quizzes.

Pretty good. Lots of work and definitely lots of speaking in the class which I don't mind but many people do. Lots of vocab as there tends to be in this sort of class. Easy for people who took French in high school.

I would not reccomend this professor at all. I would not take this class if I could go back in time. Not engaging. Not a compassionate professor, very strict. Too much busy work that doesn't help learn the material. Lectures do not engage with the material well. Exams are difficult.

analysis.name: Kaitlin Fitzgerald

You can tell Dr. Fitzgerald really cares about her work and is passionate about teaching. She understands student needs but is also fair and equitable when making decisions. She really cares and makes class genuinely interesting, you can tell she's passionate and wants us to learn, not just pass. That said, her grade cutoffs are kinda insane (a 100+ for an A+ is wild), but getting an A is totally doable. Overall, one of those professors you'll actually remember.

analysis.name: Fikri Pitsuwan

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely the first and the best econ prof I had in Cornell. Always so organized, patient, and willing to help students, whether it's during lecture or during his office hours, even over hours. Relatively hard materials solely because of math, not concept. As long as you spend time and do lots of practice, you will perform well in class.

Super interesting and widely applicable topic with a good balance between econ, game theory, and policy applications. Prof Pitsuwan is very  knowledgeable and willing to deviate from lecture to explain things more. I will say one would probably enjoy this class most if they enjoy the subject, though I think anyone could benefit from this course.

Professor Pitsuwan is one of my favorite professors at Cornell. He's incredibly supportive and very approachable. His requirements are clear, and he was always willing to help me solidify concepts. I feel like I have a strong foundation in macroeconomics and its application in the world. I highly recommend everyone take his intro macro class.

Pretty alright guy, but sort of got the sense from lecture that he hated macroeconomics. Class content is overall pretty easy so the lecture being mid aren't such a bad thing, but he writes his exams so that you actually have to think instead of just plug and chugging what you learn in class. Grading is pretty generous.

I think Prof. Pitsuwan has good intentions, and while clearly new to teaching he obviously cared about his students and the material. However, he really struggled to grasp how difficult the exams would be with exams having extreme variety in difficulty levels. Exams were infinitely harder than problems for class and very conceptual.

analysis.name: Floyd Davis
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Davis is slightly better than Hines (who taught the first half of 2070), an engaging lecturer who posts slides beforehand and is more open to questions. 2070 is a hard class regardless of professor, but Davis is a genuinely kind person and likes doing demonstrations and cracking jokes, which makes the lectures bearable. He uses slides and uploads them the night before lecture which is convenient. His examples can be slightly confusing sometimes so be sure to pay attention or else you'll be lost.

His lectures come straight from the textbook, but there are some engaging parts of lecture that add to understanding. Some homework questions appeared exactly on exams so understanding them is important. He's nice but the lectures are pretty boring. Read the textbook for anything you don't understand as he follows it exactly.

Very dependent on the textbook, everything he taught and all grading criteria came from the textbook. Often went on tangents in lectures to the point where you couldn't understand what was important and what was irrelevant. Rarely did any practice problems in class so most of us did not know how to actually apply the material.

He taught the second half of Chem2070. My biggest complaint is the homework. Hours upon hours of problem sets. The professor who taught the first half of the class gave problem sets, but Floyd gave so much more homework than she ever did.The lectures were pretty boring, but at least he made explosions to keep us engaged.

analysis.name: Fabien Royer
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward

He is very approachable and funny, and he brings that personality into his lectures.

I was surprised when this really young professor that mainly focuses on research was so effective at teaching. Thankfully, he works closely to align his course with Dr. Ritz (who has long organized this course and the exams) and the quality is about the same. Dr. Royer just takes it to the next level with his enthusiasm, care, and French accent.

Professor Royer lectures very well and is very considerate of students and student requests. I would definitely recommend going to his office hours. He is very clear on his expectations, but might occasionally miss one or two topics that you should read in the textbook or go ask about in office hours or after class.
prof is amazing with an added bonus of a cool french accent. so so so grateful i took statics with him. he made this notoriously hard class more enjoyable and accessible.

Tests are directly from problem sets. Lectures are about as unorganized as they come, and have nothing to do with the prelims. That being said, a very interesting guy. Lots of research and general worldly knowledge. Super nice and always has students' best interests. Class is fun, typical Cornell kids hate it because it's out of the box. No book :)

Go to lecture, pay attention, go to section, follow along the problems, do the problems sets. If you do all those plus a quick review (maybe 1-2 hours) before the exam you will do well. The exams are exactly like the problem sets.   Go to class, do the hw, be a good student for once and you'll do fine.
Awkward, cold and standoff-ish

Nice guy, but his lectures are disorganized and mostly irrelevant to the problem sets & tests. After everyone got an A- or above on the first exam, he put really weird, unclear questions on the second one so we wouldn't do well even if we knew the material. Can't prove equations or do arithmetic, and refuses to do any of the math in office hours.

analysis.name: Bruce Ganem
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 137 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Ganem is easily one of the best professors at this university. His lectures are direct and inspiring, and he truly gives you all the tools you need to do well in the class, if you work hard. Weekly practice is your best friend in this course. Also, pay attention in lecture, and you will be able to predict what will be big on the exams.

A lot of reviews have said that some material he tested on were not included in lecture. I disagree. Sure, some of the questions were difficult, but I would qualify them more as "thinking cap" questions, where we've seen a similar mechanism in lecture but on the test it was slightly different. TLDR, pay attention in lecture and you should be fine.

His lectures are good and cover everything you need for the test. His tests were generally fair. Curve is generous and better than gen chem. If you go for 1 on 1 office hours with Dr. Ganem, he's very condescending which doesn't help so it's best to learn what you can in lecture and use the TAs for outside help. Great teacher, meh person.

I loved Dr. Ganem even though I didn't get the best grade in honors orgo. I learned so much and he is genuinely such a cool guy. Please go to office hours to learn more about him if you have the chance because he has super cool stories and a very effective one-on-one / small group teaching style as well that you don't get from lectures.

I personally didn't find Ganem's lectures to be very helpful. Most of my learning came from taking the support class (highly recommended!), going to the learning center, and using outside resources like the organic chemistry as a second language textbook. The exams are difficult, but the curve is very generous

analysis.name: George Jakubson

review_highlights.comment: Lectures arent exactly clear but they are fairly interesting. He gives out solid practice prelims for the test and curves it generously if need be. Take ILRLE 2400 with Jakubson if you actually want to learn something. The class is curved to a B+/A-.

AVOID Prof Jakubson at ALL costs. He is the WORST. He is nice if you approach him correctly. But, his class is SO hard. The lectures and textbooks are pretty much useless. Go to office hours and take the practice prelims until you memorize the answers (exactly what he doesn't want you to do) the class is impossible if you don't. Disrespectful Prof.

By far the worst Cornell professor I've ever had and I've taken every ILR core class. Jakubson genuinely ruined my semester. It's genuinely impossible to follow the lectures/slides, and there is no textbook. He complains every class about how no one comes to class as though it's anyone but his fault.

i'm sure he is a very smart economist but he simply should not be a professor. lectures are genuinely useless but attendance counts. acts personally offended if you have a question that is "too simple." extremely condescending. exams are evil (q's are purposely misleading, include "optional" content, avg in 50s vs 90s in other classes). avoid.

So rude that I don't understand how he's still allowed to teach. It shocks me that a professor can speak to a student like this. Watching him speak to people like that made me shocked that an institution like Cornell could ever hire him. This man should not be an educator, nor exposed to most decent humans because of his unprofessionalism.

analysis.name: Adi Grabiner-Keinan
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: An aimless class with no goals or meaningful takeaways whatsoever. Weekly sessions are led by student "facilitators" and 3 hours long -- but still accomplish nothing. Tons of reading that serves no purpose b/c irrelevant or repetitive. Strict grading criteria for annoying papers. Can't discuss grades over email as they claim it's "against FERPA."

If you need to take this for your ILR diversity requirement just wait for the other diversity ethics course. this class is graded so unnecessarily harsh for no reason, one of my hardest courses for this semester and you really just don't know what they are looking for in those reflection papers. Participating in class session is easy.

Great class, show up and participate and you will do well. Fun to talk to people you wouldn't otherwise and you can see the tangible change the material facilitates within both yourself and your peers.

I took the course and then became a facilitator after. Yes, it can get tedious to read so many papers and write the weekly essays so it's supposed to not feel easy -- it's supposed to challenge students' critical thinking and reflections. The payoff is so worth it and is truly a unique experience for students from all backgrounds.

I absolutely hate this class. I have gotten C's on half of the papers. They don't care about how well you write or how deeply you incorporate the class materials -- they care about how much discrimination you face. The same 3 topics are discussed every class for 3 hours. You can't miss a section. A diversity credit should be an easy A. Do not take.

analysis.name: Martin Graef
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: An overall great professor. He explains concepts clearly and is very kind and approachable. I took him for an upper-level biology course, and exams were mostly based on lecture slides and recorded lectures. If you keep up with the material, the tests were very fair. Office hours were extremely helpful, even though not many students attended.

Martin was an excellent professor, clearly very knowledgeable and approachable during office hours. Lectures are fast-paced if you take handwritten notes, but still very engaging and fascinating. Grading is extremely fair, as exams are all MCQs and directly relate to lecture content. TAs are wonderful - take BIOMG1350 even if bio isn't your thing.

He is clearly passionate about Biology, but lectures can be fast-paced if taking handwritten notes, and kind of monotonous. Nevertheless, always accessible by OH and super knowledgable. Prelims are probably the fairest you can get--straight from lectures/slides, MCQ, do well on them and maybe skip the final. Easy class, memorization heavy!

Uninspiring to say the least. One of those classes that you take to get it over with. Nothing wrong with him but just reads off the slides. Voice could put me to sleep. Do well on the prelims and you can skip the final though.

Martin really cares about the students' learning and success. He is passionate about his research and cell biology in general. The exams were very fair, and the questions were directly from the lectures.

analysis.name: Melissa Hines
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 171 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: 2070 has historically been a bad class. Melissa did not make the class any more enjoyable. She was rude to students and the lab TAs. The lecture content was not similar to prelims and the lab reports were incredibly disorganized. If you have to take a class from her I would recommend waiting to take it from someone else.

Please do not take M Hines's class. I took it for CHEM 2070, and I fear it was extremely difficult. She sped through the lectures incredibly quickly, and she made the prelim questions so hard. She also wears the same maroon wool sweater, black pants, and cargo shoes. She does lore drop about her days at MIT, though, which was fun to hear.

Not a bad lecturer but I disliked the class. In multiple instances, she hasn't taught us a topic yet but expects to understand how to solve it in the group quizzes, and then proceeds to teach the material the day the group quiz is due. She also gave practice exams without formula sheets where you literally could not solve without the given data.

Lectures and Learning Catalytics are critically important to understand and grasp the concepts and ideas. Group Quizzes are a little more challenging but help you to master these concepts. Prelims are tricky but everything you need to know is either said by her or written as notes. Only a few TAs are helpful. Review notes daily and practice.

Hines is bad. What angers me the most is that she doesn't bother to provide formula sheets ahead of the exam when half of the practice exams posted online require knowing what values and equations were given. This created a huge pain in the butt, and her group quizzes are ridiculously long and you better pray for competent group members.

analysis.name: Kyle Harms
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 79 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: The majority of my friend group dropped this class, and I should have followed suit. The write-ups for the projects are genuinely unbearable and seem infinite. The course is listed as a web design course, but it feels like an essay-based class. Good luck getting help on Ed, where Prof. Harms response with the vaguest answers on earth. Do not take.

Without a doubt, this is by far the worst class and professor I've ever had at Cornell. He treats his students like children (not in a caring way but a patronizing one) and acts like a toddler whenever you try to talk to him. He may appear nice and act as if he cares but he's inflexible and will forsake your wellbeing for his own. Be warned.

This class is abysmal, honestly one of the worst classes I have taken at Cornell. While it may seem like a web dev class at first, the class is much more focused on unnecessary, busy-work writing than actual coding. For the first project in this class, I coded around 1-200 lines, but had to write over 3000 words to describe my "design process."

I was excited to learn about web dev at an esteemed institution such as Cornell. Kyle deserves no esteem, nor is his class a worthwhile use of time, unless you like doing arts and crafts, since all projects incorporate endless design drawing portions, which kill both trees and students' sanity. I also wrote more in this class than I did for my FWS.

INFO 2300 is a very easy class, but the thing is that Harms is just really not understanding of any extraneous situation and is really inflexible. I do not recommend this class if you have some kind of coding experience and it's not required because the web design contents covered in INFO 2300 can easily be learned over just one or two days.

analysis.name: Hadas Ritz
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 126 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Class seems to be going well at the start then all of the sudden she throws a bunch of material and assignment at us at a time that we were supposed to be on break. The material that she threw at us was not easy and was not explained at all in lecture, she simply just clicked through the slides without a care in the world for her students.

Ritz isn't the worst, but her grading with Learning Objectives (LO) is. You could be doing good all year, getting 3/3s on LOs on all 4 graded quizzes, and going into the final with 14/21 graded LOs as 3/3s and then bam. If you don't get the answer almost perfectly right you get a 1/3 and your grade is ruined. It's perfection or nothing with grades.

The new grading system is... interesting. I (and many others) did not like Ritz's course (the lectures move painfully slow), and it covers shockingly little material (no transforms??). The grading scheme doesn't aid with "learn at your own pace" and instead obfuscates what should be a pretty simple class. The material's easy, but grading isn't.

I think her teaching is fine, but most of her responses to questions on Ed Discussion are snarky and unfriendly. She will refuse to give actual answers to genuine questions and respond with things along the lines of "I will not entertain this" or "Why are you asking this?". It just makes me lose respect for her as a person.

Really clear lectures with helpful lecture notes. Not too much homework. The "outcome-based" grading system (look it up) is innovative and really rewards student effort. This professor got a bad reputation a few years ago, but has improved a ton since then. I would highly recommend taking math with them.

analysis.name: TJ Hinrichs

review_highlights.comment: unclear grading rubric with weekly quizzes
She is super nice and smart! I enjoyed her class. Thank you, Prof. Hinrichs!

Very down-to-earth and genuinely cares about her students. Sit-down discussions are very interesting but slide lectures can be a little dull. She is super supportive outside of class for a STEM student like me who is not familiar with college history courses. Would definitely take again.

TJ is really nice and she definitely cares about her students. But, her class has a unique structure that is really difficult to get used to. There is a large amount of reading to do. You discuss the readings and questions in groups and then present to everyone. Very confusing and hard essay questions. She asks you to define everything. Participate

DO NOT TAKE! Wait one semester! She requires mandatory attendance and makes you wear name tags and assigned seating as if we are in middle school. analysis.name: Donna Haeger

It was absolutely unnecessary
Grade is based off whether or not you have a good TA. Some are stricter than others. Prelims are unorganized, but manageable if you watch her videos.

he cares about the content and the field, but he should not teach. he mumbles all through the lectures, its extremely hard to keep up with what he is saying and he just makes the already hard course even more impossible to pass. analysis.name: Ileen DeVault

Devault was the most biased Prof I had at Cornell. Teaches 'historical facts' but implies clear political ideology. DeVault is one of the nicest people I have ever met. She genuinely cares about her students. I had a class during her office hours, so she regularly scheduled other times to meet. I struggled a lot with the first essay assignment, but DeVault helped me and taught me what I did wrong. Her lectures can be dry, but the information is interesting.

Awesome professor, super passionate and nice.  Lots of weekly reading with reading quizzes in discussion, but you can definitely "skim" main concepts and do extremely well. Two papers, go to TA office hours and you will do well on them. Extremely fair final. Some find the subject boring overall but DeVault makes it as painless as possible.

analysis.name: Immanuel Trummer

He might be good at researching, but he is absolutely not good at lecturing and breaking things down. If you take his class, be ready to learn on your own for more than 80% of the time. Even many CS lovers on YouTube outperform him in terms of teaching DB stuff.

This class was terrible. Legit could win an award for how badly he teaches. The content isn't necessarily hard, but his extreme lack of explanation and his terribly worded problem sets make it unnecessarily difficult. To top it off, the course staff he hires for this class have absolutely no idea what they are doing and mess up when they help.
The content of the course is really useful. However, his lecture is hard to understand even with the video recordings from past semester.

Clear, consistent, and actually cares for his students. Dr. Lorey will get you through Chem1560 as long as you are willing to put in the work.
THE MOST BORING LECTURES EVER YOU WILL FALL ASLEEP,  The way he teaches is so boring and make you go to sleep. Every time I'm there I fall asleep, not only me but many students

The class starts out pretty easy, but after the first prelim, picks up speed pretty quickly. Dr. Lorey is a great professor, if something isn't clear to you during class, hes good for in class questions and provides plenty of office hours for you to get help.

Class itself was not that difficult just incredibly boring and required you to do an obnoxious amount of reading which was NEVER talked about in class so you had to infer meanings on the tests. This course was honestly more difficult than CHEM 2070 lecture

Does not respond to emails and will not release rubrics so students are left guessing what they did right and what they should keep on doing for following labs. Does not provide rubric for labs to students or TAs, so there is no way to improve performance. Grades on things never mentioned before, then tells students not to submit regrades. Leaves comments on reports that is not constructive, but critical and unsupportive.

analysis.name: Justin Murfin

The assignments and exams were very vague as he is more focused on his research and academic drama.

She knows her stuff, exams are tough, and she cares a lot. She's super pretty, so it's a bonus if u get bored with the lecture. Findley the TA is the goat.

Definitely a difficult course but also the readings can be easily skimmed, and the writing assignments like Kheel Center/Film review are interesting/grade boosters. TA is super chill. Final seems really difficult though, may change in future.

Really nice person. Only a couple of assignments, 2 Exams. 30% of the class is participation. Very long readings and a very hard final exam. What I appreciate most about Dr. Modica is that she's open to change in how she formats readings, assignments, and exams.

She has a lot on the lecture slides and expects everyone to be familiar with what's on the slide and in the readings. The hardest part is that she doesn't make it clear what will realistically come up again in the course; best piece of advice is go to office hours as she tends to be a lot more clear there.

analysis.name: Hyun-ho Joo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
