Kind professor, really cares about the material. Not much to prepare for class other than doing the readings. Interesting and low-stakes class

Prof was so kind and cared so much about the material. Class was a mix of lecture and discussion, he really wants people to participate. Lectures basically reiterate the readings, which aren't too heavy. Not many assignments in the class, and prompts are given in advance so you can prepare your essays ahead of time.

In an hour and a half, Prof. Joo's lectures accomplish what could otherwise be achieved in three sentences. He has good intentions and the readings are interesting, but the class itself mostly consisted of him reading excerpts of what we were already expected to read.

If you care about your GPA, don't take this class. He has extremely difficult content in problem sets (6 a semester), after lecture quizzes, and most of the time he couldn't finish his lecture.

His class IS difficult. the PSs are pretty long but good practice for the tests. He really cares for his students and WILL try his best to help you if you reach out, and he's genuinely passionate about what he teaches. To get good grades, make sure you understand the concepts and do the sections and really listen to the lectures.

This class is genuinely brutal. Just one of those classes you need to get through for Econ major but it's frustrating the entire time. P-sets are actually absurd and the entire course is multi variable calculus despite half the class not having taken that class. Prof seems like a good dude but it doesn't really matter when the class is unreasonable

He's good at explaining things in lecture. He's caring and always willing to answer questions during class and after. It's a tough class and problem sets are long but the work is helpful for exam prep. If you have to take this class, I would recommend taking his!

Sweet professor who cares about course and students, but very difficult course. P-sets would take upwards of 8 hours each (6 p-sets for the course and one drop). Exams are much easier than p-sets. Lectures are difficult to follow, but posts lecture slides. Accessible for OH. Would recommend, but be ready to put in a lot of work outside of lectures

analysis.name: Katrina Nousek

It's unfortunate because it means that we are not making good use of our tuition/time/energy here at Cornell.

If you choose to take a class by this professor... don't. If you have to take a class from this professor... good luck. She is hard to listen to and rants for ages. The class has no clear rubrics or standards for writing. She grades harshly with little to no room for improvement no matter how much time you may spend. Save yourself, please.

Such an interesting class and the professor is clearly well informed (rambles a bit too much). Only issue is she barely gives out As and doesn't give proper feedback. Super nice during office hours and overall a great teacher.

analysis.name: Vikram Krishnamurthy

The best sophomore class at Cornell I have taken. Prof Krishnamurthy is inspirational in the class. He went out of the way to  explain the assignments in the office hours. I strongly recommend this course as an ENGRD requirement. The assignments are difficult and time consuming but u learn a lot. Prof. K really knows his stuff.

(FA2021) He spent a lot of time on how very basic calculus he would re-teach (a course prerequisite) is graduate material and that the course material was invaluable for interviews. He taught algorithms by putting code onto slides in one giant PDF. Generous grader, but stressful since you need to rely on Google to complete homework.

I did this class in spring 2024 and enjoyed it a lot. He is a very smart guy, and he is very caring too. ECE 2720 is offered both semesters but the instructor changes each semester. One of the best ENGRD courses I have done

This is an excellent and mathematically heavily rigorous course. I am a CS-major.  I did not know that linear algebra had so many real world applications. This guy seriously rocks, he is very approachable. The assignment take a lot of time. But no prelims :)  One of the best courses at Cornell by far. Final exam will be take-home.

She is a bit disorganized in her lectures and it can be hard to follow. Her tests mainly surround the slideshows but I would use the textbook as extra information. Don't listen to the past reviews!! She has definitely taken feedback to make her lectures more organized and is a very kind teacher :) Give her a chance!

Leah teaches college students like an elementary school teacher. She will wait till everyone stops talking, spends most of the lecture talking about inclusivity, and rushes through the overall material. Avoid her at all costs.

Great lectures and great lecture notes. Really supportive professor who is very willing to help outside of class. Provides practice prelims very similar to real ones. However, homework assignments are long and very harshly graded.
Lectures were really disorganized, hard to follow, and unprepared. Exams were mostly easy enough to compensate. Homeworks took a lot of work and didn't really feel that beneficial for learning.

analysis.name: Lawrence Shuster

prof shuster loves to cold call and make targeted and passive aggressive comments when students are struggling with the material. he seems to insist it's all very easy even though it is in fact quite difficult at times and he doesn't really explain most of the material. he's also very disorganized and made some weird comments about autism and women

A very eccentric professor, wants the class to be free and open but grades harshly when you do not specifically respond to the ridiculous questions that occur to her when reading your writing. analysis.name: Kyle Lancaster

Dr. Lancaster is, hands down, one of the best professors you'll ever have in any Chemistry class at Cornell. Always willing to help students in office hours and was a DJ at one point in time.

Super knowledgable guy, very engaging lectures. Homework assignments are long and challenging but very well-written and overall enjoyable. Available both during stated office hours and at other times, and by email.

If there is one class I could retake at Cornell, it is CHEM 2070: General Chemistry I with Dr. Lancaster. Not only is he hilarious and a chemistry legend, but he also has deep respect for his students. He actually wants to know his students and help them achieve their goals. These other reviews must be lying because BIOG1500 is not a great course. It insists upon itself and is a tad bit pretentious. Nothing against the professor, it's just that the course is a lot of busy work and not very informative.

analysis.name: Matthew Paskell

However, he made the class discussions pretty interesting and gave really helpful feedback on the essays, so the class was worth taking.

Paskell was a pretty chill professor. Five essays in total, with each being weighted more as the semester progresses. Attendance in lectures and discussions were mandatory, but going is helpful just to gain further clarification on the readings and insights to write the essays.

analysis.name: Matthew Wilkens

No curve. Cares more about the TAs than the actual students. Boring lecturer. Truly the worst experience ever with a professor. The final was uncharacteristically hard and he really doesn't seem to care about the student. He shouldn't teach this course!

He was extremely caring, made sure the teaching staff was flexible, and actively sought out and implemented feedback.

Professor Wilkens is indubitably the most caring and passionate professor I have met. His lectures are engaging, and he genuinely cares about students' understanding. Both the midterm and the final were very similar to the practice tests, so everything felt very fair. He clearly puts effort into making the course run smoothly and explains the material well. The homework was lengthy, but it helped reinforce the concepts. Overall, he is a strong instructor and I learned a lot from the class.

analysis.name: Robert Newman

This class is a joke. You drag yourself to an 8am for zero useful info—just nonsense rants and breakdowns. He warns against "amdalga breakdowns" but literally has them himself. Tests are garbage and make no sense. Haven't learned a single thing. Absolute waste of time.

Would not recommend! The amount of work assigned is going to be way more compared to other classes with similar credit levels, and the lectures are not engaging enough for how early they are.  Slides and homework have typos, and you don't need to take this class to learn what is taught.

Self-centered and arrogant and treats his students like children while rarely offering insightful answers to genuinely meaningful questions. For a class so centered on emotional intelligence, I think he could benefit more from taking this course himself than any student enrolled currently. This course is a waste of time and money.

It takes a special type of arrogance to believe you deserve the undivided attention of 50+ people from 8-10AM. He practices what he preaches, seems to care about students. A bajillion assignments going on at all times. In a huge group all sem, felt ridiculous begging adults to study for a test because my grade depended on theirs (group average).

analysis.name: Nick Salvato

review_highlights.comment: He was one of the best professor I could have asked for to introduce me to the PMA department.

Professor Salvato is one of the most compassionate professors I have encountered at Cornell. He is a great lecturer and passionate about PMA, which makes his class very enjoyable. He also pays great attention to how his students are doing as a whole, making constant efforts to check in. If the opportunity presents itself, take one of his classes!

Nick is perhaps the best lecturer at Cornell - he doesn't hesitate, break, or stutter through entire classes. He also remembers students' names within weeks in large lectures. He's also one of the clearest critical thinkers on campus. Not an easy grader, but definitely fair

I took Television with him (AMST 2160) and he was such a great teacher, the class clapped for him after the first and last lecture. We watched films throughout the lectures and talked about them. We were also assigned to watch these for homework and sometimes they'd be a little long. Essay grading was a little tough but doable if you put in effort

Lectures were fine, but he likes to hear himself talk, I'd reccomend going and paying attention, but this class didn't require that much work. It just wasn't as interesting as I hoped it would be. Essays are fairly light and the HW isn't bad, just keep up with it and put a little more effort than you might expect.

analysis.name: Kristoffer Nimark

Grading was very inconsistent across different students. No incentive to attend class as he brought 0 enthusiasm and effort to his lectures. Said he'd “curve the class” and yet there was no curve.

Content presented in a boring, uninspiring way. Good 4000 level econ class if you don't want to overwork yourself - no required attendance, exam material can be found on the slides. His teaching methods reflects that he doesn't think having a super difficult class is necessary - which can be seen as time-wasting to some and low-stress to others.
seriously the worst lecturer i have ever had. trails off at the end of every sentence and barely looks up from his screen. also very reluctant to answer questions

This guy straight up doesnt care. Very uninspiring lecturer, shows up late from time to time and doesnt help at all. analysis.name: George Orlov
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 54 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Horrible lecturer, overcomplicates very simple topics to the point where you have no idea what he's talking about. Posts slides onto canvas but they do not help. Get ready to read the entire textbook if you want to know what's going on. Also assigns way too many useless homework assignments a week (50+ questions), and the prelims are difficult.

It can be a confusing course, and in Orlov's lectures, you need to pay attention to little things and the interaction between topics. You can miss up to 6 classes without losing attendance points, but sometimes it takes a while for lecture/discussion slides and lecture recordings to be uploaded. Two midterms and a final based on practice prelims.

He was my lab instructor and miscalculated my total grade back in 2015, which became a source of long-lasting frustration for me. 

I still remember how proud he seemed of himself, and how he carried that unnecessary arrogance during lab sessions. 

I feel justified making this comment now because I am currently a PhD candidate.

PollEv was kinda annoying, but other than that, he was a super chill prof who had some fun references in his exams. His lectures really told you everything you needed to know about the exams, and the slides were my best friend. One or two things seemed subjective on exams, however.

Orlov gets a lot of unjustified hate. As long as you actually go to class, the material is super understandable. He genuinely cares about making sure students know the content and his slides are very thorough. He also provides plenty of extra resources and ample help hours for extra practice. The tests are exactly like the practice exams.

analysis.name: Ted O'Donoghue
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 31 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor O'Donoghue is an outstanding professor, and his lectures were always engaging. This is one of the most interesting and rewarding classes I have taken in the econ major. O'Donoghue is truly at the forefront of this field and often cites his own research which is fascinating. Very difficult course, but lectures are amazing and p-sets and exams are fair if you are keeping up. Would highly recommend for all majors.

O'Donoghue is kind, knowledgeable, and engaging. Behavioral economics is not an easy course, but O'Donoghue articulates concepts clearly and provides lots of materials to help. Highly recommend! Just make sure your schedule isn't too overwhelming when you add 4660 - there's a lot of material.

Extremely organized and handled the logistics of this 5-professor course. Super clear in explaining concepts and satisfyingly concise slides. I had no prior econ education but still managed to understand everything. Also helpful during office hours: an incredibly intelligent professor!

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. AEM 2020/PSYCH 2940 was so difficult for no reason. Prelims and the final are 100% of your grade and the tests are extremely hard. For a class that does not even count towards your major, it is not worth it.

analysis.name: Peggy Odom-Reed

Even though we are in a pandemic, and she can't even make it to class due to her health issues, she expects her students to treat this class like their only obligation. She has no empathy for others. She does not give positive comments. She does not give A's. She's the worst professor.

This class is impossible to do well in. Only take it if you like to cry. Prepare to have your spirit decimated, your motivation demolished, and your will to live diminished. Turn around time is so fast on assignments that you quite literally will either have to fail all your other classes or take the L. Dont do it. Unless you hate yourself.

I genuinely wonder if she is aware that we take multiple other classes. Atrocious, merciless, insufferable, mental-breakdown-inducing, brutal, cruel, unrelenting, unendurable, overwhelming. Prof Ozanne cares about what she teaches and makes every class interesting, even at 8:40 in the morning. Would definitely take her again.

You can tell that she is very passionate about F&B. Her prelims were all really fair. Everything she told us to study for (during our review lecture) was on the prelim and nothing more. There was a slight problem with wording in a few of her questions but other than that her class is very doable.
Great person, great teacher. I was not interested in food and beverage when I came into this class. Left the class somewhat interested in it. Nothing but great things to say about her
she cares about her students and she really likes the subject. however, she's french...

I took Dr. Olson's online Sustainable Preservation series but that's not an option to select. She was great, very approachable, knowledgeable and provided substantive feedback.

Worst Professor I have had by far in the CRP department. Grading was unfair, never returned in a timely manner, and harsh. Let me put in caveperson speak so clear. Much read. Professor talk many word, say nothing. Sassy. Condescending. Self-center. Grade tough for fun. TA ridiculous. Guest speaker come. Professor no care. Me no care. Give test on thing never teach. Exam long. Time short. Me still confused. Me learn by self. Professor bald. That all.

His foundations class is an absolute joke. All Dyson freshmen are required to take this course and he grades way too harshly for someone who doesn't even teach. The class is just guest speakers who come in every class (aside from prelim days).

He doesn't teach the class. Loved the guest lectures but if he's gonna have prelims in this class, they need to be based off guest lectures, not an external reading that makes no sense. Grading is so vague. Got a 60 on the first prelim and 80 on the 2nd one. This class should go back to being pass/fail. Students skipped anyway and chatted prelim.
Very dependent on exams. Prelims are based off reading that is outside of the class. Tough grader and purely gives grade based on whether or not he knows who you are.

I see him at parties, what a guy. Not a guy during lectures :

8th grade math yo, actually though just CASE out
he's impossible to understand and lectures about everything we don't need to know but leaves out everything we do. Sometimes it's actually funny how confusing he can be

analysis.name: Kelly Pohland
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward

She also gave us a short break in the middle of lecture to go over logistics like deadlines and tests. This helped us to stay organized and refresh us in the middle of class.

Overcomplicates simple calc 2 concepts. Homework also gets longer and complicated for no reason. She also isn't transparent about class mean and median which makes it difficult to gauge how you're doing. There are quizzes in every discussion so it's mandatory to attend.

If her class is open, then take it. She is an amazing lecturer. As a student who did not take BC in high school, she was very thorough in teaching new concepts. She is also very accessible during OHs.

Professor Pohland was really nice in this class was just sometimes unnecessarily difficult. Homework is pretty hard but doable. The discussion was not helpful because the TA was going through a worksheet really fast. Everything is graded very picky.
Lectures were alright. Would often spend way too much time answering one person's questions. Tests were hard, and they never gave practice representative of the actual test.

analysis.name: Sun Peidong

review_highlights.comment: went into the class expecting to learn from her but every class is a presentation on the reading due that day from a student. already disappointed. on top of that, she interrupts student presentations so nitpick irrelevant things and go on tangents using metaphors that barely connects to the conversation. didnt learn anything from her. disappoined.

Worst history class I've ever taken. Expectations/criteria unclear and changed syllabus as late as March;as a result, grading is based on vibes. Graded on an interview+presentation; you aren't taught how to do any of these things. Expects high amount of background knowledge on subject matter. Very disorganized class, with no clear direction. Avoid.

She dumped the course load on students with zero guidance, then acted like giving actual feedback was beneath her. And let's not forget the unprovoked yelling,

Add on: she will give you NO indication of grades, bullies students who are presenting, and bases grading on whether she likes you. I have an above 4.0 GPA and this class single-handedly ruined my prospects of summa cum laude. She does not teach whatsoever, instead relying on students to give presentations on articles written by other scholars.

If you like your GPA, avoid this class. Professor does not communicate grading standards at all, and your end of semester grade is seemingly random. She likes to bully students in class, and teaches nothing but expects students to already know everything. If you like Maoism, borrow a book from the library. Do not take this class.

analysis.name: Pedro Perez

review_highlights.comment: He is extremely passionate about his subject.  His quizzes are fairly difficult, especially if you don't read the textbook.  Also, TA grading can be fairly subjective.  I highly recommend going to TA office hours to talk about cases.  Overall a great and informative class!  Pedro knows a lot and is willing to share if you speak with him.

The class is ridiculous. Title business management and organization yet the class is anything but well organized. The professor rambles on with zero intention and the classroom is full of students distracted by anything else. The cases are a ridiculous assignment that you have to learn exactly how to write. No clue why this is a mandatory course.

Super passionate lectures about NOTHING. You will not learn anything in this course. He just rambles on with no direction, and asks the most nonsensical questions during discussions. No idea why this class even exists. Waste of time

Mr. Perez is an extremely enthusiastic lecturer, and the emotion in his voice makes his teaching powerful and engaging. The class is largely run by the TAs—you are assigned two who oversee your cohort of about 20 students. The course has weekly case studies focused on real-world business problems, all graded with a high level of detail.

The professor himself is nice, but basically he isn't involved in the class whatsoever other than giving lectures where he rambles on about nothing really. The biggest problem with this class is the grading system, where your entire grade rests on the TAs. The final project is horrible—TAs said we could try regrade but then brought the grade down.

analysis.name: Meng Qi
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible

review_highlights.comment: BS from Tsinghua; PhD from UC Berkeley. She's “nice,” but the flipped classroom feels like buz 💻, which is expected. No rubric, so assignments are unclear and feedback is vague (“minus 1 point for blah”). Individual and class assignments are tough; the curve spans all sections—think twice before sharing what's on the exam 🫵🫵🫵 no partial credit

Did not learn a single thing. Never taught in class, not once. Misinformed me about when the last day of class would be and made me miss a crucial assignment. Then when asked about it, her response was that she believed it didn't matter since I "wouldn't have done well on it anyway". None of my peers nor I took a single thing away from this class.

For how ever many supposed "degrees" she has, she actually might be one of the least competent profs I've ever met. She has zero common sense. She makes tests intentionally hard, tricky, & unfair. Just know that email-her and in-person-her are two completely separate personalities. Email version chooses violence. IRL version is sweet as pie.

Conniving. Her flipped classroom method is very ineffective. Class starts really easy and gets unimaginably complex very fast. She seems nice but don't be fooled, she's not. She doesn't know how to teach because she herself just finished school. Sections get treated differently, you'll see why. Just try to keep up and work with others

Flipped-classroom. You copy videos for homework, then do a harder version of the content in class. No explanation of the material. In-class TAs do not grade and cannot provide clear feedback or help, and the grading criteria is unclear. Students submit identical work and receive different scores. Assignments due @ the end of class. A waste of time.

analysis.name: Ernesto Quinonez
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ernesto was the most laid back professor I've ever had and this was probably the most chill class I've ever taken. Also the second half of class you watch movies once a week and discuss the other day, final project is also a 5 min video on any topic you want. Goated class.
Friendly, but provides no useful feedback and no help improving work. Doesn't pay much attention. Easy grader but you learn nothing.

He won't sugar-coat, but he will make you a better, more aware writer. And if he pulls you aside to encourage you to send your work out... you know you've made it :)

Ernesto was my professor for True Stories (FWS). He is a great and will really work with students. He assigns few papers and gives good feedback. The class is about radical political movements in the US, and we sometimes watched movies and did light readings (less than 5 pgs). Would 10/10 recommend this class to anyone!!

Super laid-back. Classes with him are really relaxed. He points out great writing advice and tips I never knew about. He's just not too into fantasy writing and head's up: not good with remembering names. I've taken two classes with him.

analysis.name: Cheryl Qian
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

She helped us get our findings published, present at conferences, and advocated for us at every step of the way. Can't say enough about her.

Dr. Qian was definitely challenging, but she's incredibly supportive and always willing to talk through ideas or give thoughtful feedback.

By the end of the semester, I felt like I had leveled up as a designer and as a thinker. If you're ready to put in the work, she will absolutely meet you there and help you get the most out of the process.

Dr. Qian is one of the most intellectually stimulating professors I've worked with at Cornell. She pushes us to question assumptions and find design solutions that are both elegant and ethical. She also genuinely cares about her students' growth. 

She gives tough feedback, but it's always constructive and deeply insightful.
She's the best professor I've ever had.
Probably the best professor at Cornell. She's a great mentor, accessible, and willing to work with you on every level. Taking her class redefined my entire life and career.

Very personally invested in teaching and improving every student's Chinese skills, though that sometimes means being harsh with her students and assigning large amounts of memorization-heavy homework. Pretty strict but wants you to improve. Skilled at teaching the material and getting conversations going. In class exercises are repetitive but improved pronunciation a ton. Sizable daily HW + daily writing quizzes. When you make a mistake, she is strict with you, making you repeat until you get it correct, which can be stressful (Whiplash vibes).

YOU need to forget about what your $10000 career coach tells you. Just go to class and learn!!!

He'll give you an A if you do better than everyone else as its subjective at the end of the semester. (grad students tend to be bricks and bring grade average down)

Professor Quan tricks you the first day. He makes fun of his grades on rate my professor which makes you think that they are fake however they are real and he is one of the worst professors at Cornell. Do everything you can to avoid him.

He is terrible at answering questions.  His exams are manageable, but nonetheless difficult.

Professor Qian is one of the worst professors I've had. Hard to understand lectures. Prelims are ridiculous true/false questions that don't test actual understanding of material. Very rude! the whole course is a mess and won't teach you much stuff. The professors and TAs are unhelpful. You'd rather self-study instead of taking this class

Really good class but super challenging, def got a lot out of the class. In class quizzes were fairly easy as long as you paid attention in class, and the final was fair. Would recommend starting HWs later as he often makes edits because he realized problems are way too hard.

The class is pretty easy, lots of different assignment styles, which break up the rigidness unlike other Cornell Science classes. Each lecture is pretty boring, and Prof Ryan is not the most well spoken. There are farm field trips instead of traditional labs that are fun and inspiring. Midterm and Final were both straight forward.

Professor Ryan is a great guy and is very accessible. The TA's were very nice and helpful as well. The class is easy as long as you turn things in on time. The only downside was the 3 hour labs outside on farms during freezing rain or snow.

analysis.name: Ryan Tapping

review_highlights.comment: Super sweet and enthusiastic guy. You can genuinely tell teaching is a passion, and he does it well! Always receptive to feedback and willing to help everyone. Class isn't as easy as everyones making it out to be though, its still pretty difficulty, ESPECIALLY when magnetism is introduced. So participate in ur COOPs and stay on top of it :-)

Ryan Tapping is by far my favorite professor at Cornell. He cares so much about all of his students, and is truly enthusiastic about what he teaches. It is easy to do well in his class, because 45% of your grade is participation/homework. The exams are very doable. I hope Cornell recognises professor Tapping for his exceptional teaching.

Dr. Tapping is an amazing professor. He is truly one of a kind. I have so much respect for him. He made phys2213 enjoyable even though it was a hard class. His office hours were very helpful, and he is very patient with students. Thank you for everything Dr. Tapping and to all students taking his class in the future, please be nice to him.

He is a really nice guy and puts in the effort to make a nice learning environment. He seems to really care about his students and goes out of his way to try to make his lectures more enjoyable. The work is not light though its a physics course so you do get a bunch of homework and everything else that comes with a physics course.

Tapping is the kindest and fairest professor you can get at Cornell. Lectures are easy to follow along, and he makes every class enjoyable. You will LOVE this class. It's not too hard to get a B in the class, but DO NOT slack off.

analysis.name: Stephanie Thomas

Disorganized + careless. Professor mishandled the final: some students seemed to start 30–40 min early, discussed questions, took photos, and bragged about ChatGPT with no enforcement. Exam was nothing like practice/MindTap. Lectures felt rushed, questions dismissed, expectations unclear all semester. Grading felt arbitrary too. Avoid at all costs.

Her wait time for grades is absolutely ridiculous sometimes. Plus, her tests are also very irrelevant to what we're actually taught in the course so it's very easy to wait a long time for a frustratingly bad grade. Overall though, she's not a bad teacher.

God awful professor who does not care about her students. She purposely sets her students up to do terrible. Doesn't give enough time on exams and her exams are "open note" but you have no time to check the notes adequately.

Her lecture quality and teaching is fine, but she needs a lot of work on the grading aspect. People learn econ pretty well, yet only 3 exams are given and they're mostly irrelevant so most people do bad, thus their grades inaccurately represents their knowledge. Plus, she has the longest wait time for grades I've ever experienced for a professor.

Widely held as the worst professor in the PAM department. Class is dreadful. She totally picks favorites, which affects your grade. Her name tells part of the story because she is quite "sassy," as in rude. The grading of her assignments makes no sense, yet the assignments will be graded harshly. Good luck figuring out how not to lose points.

If 20+ people show up to a lecture every day just to click in and then leave immediately, something is wrong. Sassler is the worst prof I've had in the PAM department, and if this class had been my first exposure to the major I probably would have switched out of it. Painful, useless class that was torture to sit through twice a week.

analysis.name: Tracy Mitrano

However, I thought that this class was helpful in addressing the societal impact of AI. Mitrano is so passionate and willing to meet students where they are.

Gives out grades without justifications. Let us debate all the time so she can do nothing and just be lazy. Talks randomly and writes down random things on board with no organization. Biased toward immigrants and minorities. Super mean and subjective. Save your time please, don't take her class.

analysis.name: Toby Ault
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I took Dr. Ault's EAS 1330 and 1310 courses. Dr. Ault explains the material in a way that's easy to understand for both majors and non-majors. He offers plenty of extra credit and is overall a great professor. Easy A, definitely take him for distributions. He was also recently nominated for the CALS Chancellor's Award for Excellence in Teaching!

This is a guy you would WANT to find outside lecture. He cares about his students' experiences just as much as the lecture material. Although he cracks jokes and likes to make students laugh a lot, his anecdotes and rambles tie into class material, creating cohesive stories that teach the material at a competent and memorable level.

This review is actually for EAS 1300. He's one of the greats at Cornell. He wants all of his students to succeed, only ones that wouldn't are who to refuse to show to class. Take a class of his not only for a requirement, but if you need to reach 120 college credits. Lectures were awesome. Not much homework either. He is the GOAT

AWESOME PROF. Prof Ault understands most people are taking this class for distribution and doesn't try to make the class too hard. Genuinely, he just wants to make people understand the threat of climate change and need for action. Rewards people who come to class with extra credit opportunities. Tries to make lectures engaging and class not hard.

For an easy A class that fufilled two of the annoying Arts & Science distributions at once, Professor Ault understood the assignment. He knew most people didn't care about it and was taking the class because they had to, so he made the lectures interesting within that context. He was fun and gave tech breaks, absolute G!

analysis.name: Tom Gilovich
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 76 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: My favorite class at Cornell, definitely take it. So interesting and applicable to daily life. Gilovich is a rock star in the psych world and he knows all the material by heart + wrote the textbook. Both prelims and the final are horribly specific. Read the textbook word for word. Attendance not required but will definitely help you on the test

PSYCH 2800 was a really interesting course. Lecture is definitely super important - he would include niche comments from lecture on tests. Top 3 class I've taken at Cornell. If you are interested in the class content, the grades will follow. Would not recommend taking it as a non-psych major for fun/distribution, unless S/U. His outfits also eat!

One of the best psych classes I've taken at Cornell. Attendance not mandatory but his lectures are always super interesting and I agree with others that say he has the best fits. Tests did include a lot of specific facts from readings/lecture, but nothing too unmanageable.

Would not take this class unless it's required - big mistake as someone who's not a psych major and just thought the subject matter would be interesting. He's a good lecturer but the grade is 80% based on tests, so makes it very hard to do well if you're not a strong test taker.

Not an easy class, and I wouldn't recommend taking it just for fun. The material is interesting, especially if you have an interest in the social sciences, but the class structure is a bit strict and most of your grade is from exams that are quite difficult. I've taken several Psych/HD courses, and this was definitely one of the hardest ones.

analysis.name: Tejasvi Nagaraja
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Honestly, this is an amazing class. Take it. It has a pretty simple format: one midterm, two essays, and some in-class assignments/ quizzes. He also gave an option for extra credit. Every assignment allows you to explore what YOU are interested in. He is so flexible,open to connecting with students beyond the classroom, so passionate. Trust me-TAKE

Honestly he is amazing. This course you get to explore so much of what you are interested in. I wish he explored more Latin history. But he is so much fun, love the music he plays in class and the topics we discuss. Really dynamic format. Super great. Easy if you try on assignments. Trust!.
Great guy, boring lectures. your grade relies on your TA.

Lectures were all over the place, he YAPPS about irrelevant information 80% of the class. We had 6-7 different books to read almost at random across the semester. Papers are extremely long for an intro class, and even longer readings. Extremely boring lectures, only survived because of my TA! (They are 100% responsible for all the grading).

All papers. Your grade lives and dies by the TA. This semester, there was a probably a 1.5 gpa spread between the two TAs and nothing was done about it. Your grade is about as meritocratic as a roulette table. Lectures are circular, directionless, and unnecessary. Just read the pertinent readings a week before the papers are due.

analysis.name: Ulrich Wiesner
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Funny dude - I really enjoyed his lectures. The course does not cover much content so discussions were constant review, didn't have to spend much time outside of class (please still study though). Exams were straightforward. You are set up to succeed in the class - wanted to put this review so students are deterred from taking his class, he's good.

He is one of the worst professors I have ever had at Cornell.  The guy does not care about his students, never answers emails, barely gives any homework (which makes it impossible to study) and is so boring in lecture.  I think he really doesn't care if you do well or not and makes his tests really hard.  He also has awful handwriting.

Great lecturer excited about the material that tries to make sure that all students learn in his class. It is an interesting and enjoyable class if you stay engaged, as he also definitely cares about participation. Class doesn't have too much homework but requires paper reading. I and other friends all had positive experiences

Very crappy professor. No website, not enough homeworks given, poor handwriting, and no assistance before examinations. I honestly think that he is one of the most lazy and unmotivated professors who simply does not care whether or not you learn anything. I learnt nothing and he sucks.
The worst professor ever in my life.

analysis.name: Madeleine Udell
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Rarely am I compelled to post a review on Rate My Professor but this class was so horrible and utter garbage that I must insist. The class is hard for no reason at the expensive of learning, I can probably find more help from Scooby Doo than the Professor and TA team. There is no transparency in grading. Don't take any of her classes stay far away.

Udell's explanations were so unclear to the point that I had to google everything she said - the Internet did a much better job than her in teaching. Grading was not transparent - hw instructions and grading rubric were very vague. She made us do peer grading - this coupled with non-transparent grading incentivized students to sabotage their peers.

I find it difficult to believe that Cornell University would include this professor in its team of excellent professors. You can just skip class because the lectures are worthless. Support from TAs and Professors are also non-existent. The group projects are graded with unsupervised peer review, incentivizing people to sabotage others. Don't take.

The project has no rubric no matter how much you ask. Then you get a random grade a few peers come up with. Your project can be better than others but you will get a worse grade. learn very little in this class. Avoid this class please. Its just a shame there are not other good ML classes available. it forces people to take this class.

Probably one of the worst classes I've taken at Cornell. Udell is a confusing lecturer, and the TAs for the class are very focused on not giving you the answers to problem sets, even if it comes at the expense of a student's understanding. Very unclear and somewhat arbitrary grading rubrics. Avoid at all costs.

analysis.name: Christopher "Kit" Umbach
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: exactly what others talk about. He expects too much prior knowledge about chem and physics even if its engri class and the class is really boring and long.

In MSE, you will probably have to take a decent amount of classes with Umbach. He is pretty not well-received as he doesn't teach well and tends to give grades arbitrarily based on personal feeling. You probably won't learn much in his classes and will def feel frustrated, but if you BS effort and try to seem nice, you'll get a better grade...

Umbach, although tough, is an entertaining lecturer and a kind professor. Arguably, Nanotechnology is one of the hardest ENGRI classes, and as such, many people are disappointed with workload. However, the class was inspirational, and it completely changed my personal career path; as such, I highly recommend the class for motivated individuals

Decent professor. His lecture notes are extremely dense, but he posts all the power points which are very convenient for reviewing for prelims. For the most part the tests are fair. The homeworks didn't seem particularly useful but they weren't very hard. The material is often interesting, other times not so much. Overall a decent experience
horrible teacher. expects you to know too much. ridiculous tests and unfair grading policies. do not take nanotech!

analysis.name: Audrey Ukhov
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: GREAT Professor. had him for advanced corporate finance. new guy, but dont be scared, he knows what he is talking about and has a genuine interest in the students. Very helpful at office hours, and makes advanced corporate finance interesting. Oh, his random facts about the finance world are the great. All hail Buffet!
Legit professor of finance. hotel school sucks less with real finance professors like ukhov
Great professor who cares about his students, especially  if you go to class and get to know him. Loves athletes. His lectures tend to be long and a bit boring but great professor overall.

My favorite professor at Cornell thus far. Extremely smart and knowledgable, and also willing to help his students in office hours. Go to office hours and get to know him-- he's a great guy with funny stories, and it will help you in the class. If you show up and pay attention, no reason you can't do well.

This guy is hilarious. Has a heavy Russian accent, which makes his stories even funnier. Cares that you learn the material. Exams are much harder than the practice problems and homework he gives. But all in all, one of the best professors I've had simply for the pure entertainment value of his stories.

analysis.name: Uri Possen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had the privilege of taking three classes with the late Professor Uri Possen, including an independent study. He was one of my best and favorite professors at Cornell. He taught financial economics, a subject I loved. He helped me sign up for an internship, because of which I got the visa papers. Always there for his students. I miss you. RIP!

He doesn't explain the models in detail during the class, rather you should study on the textbook. However, he and his TA like to help students preparing the exams. He provides good insights on the current macroeconomic situation especially on fiscal policy and financial economics . Generous grader.

Good Professor. Very interesting course. Go to all lectures and take good notes; cross-reference with the book and go to office hours for whatever you don't get, he'll appreciate you all the more. Start the paper assignment EARLY!!

I had literally already taken finance when I took this class, so it was kind of a joke. But he's not a bad guy. Strangely though, I got a higher grade in the Johnson school finance class I took previously than I did this one. Odd.
His lecture is a bit tedious, but he is personally nice guy and always willing to help students. When you rely on textbook, which is the best i have ever seen, you can ace this course

analysis.name: David Usher
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His exams sometimes tested things that were specific examples during lecture. There are 3 prelims, 1 final, and no homework. This saves you time, but also gives you less of a grade buffer. I hate orgo, and even though he was entertaining with singing and stories, I couldn't wait for the semester to end. His digressions got annoying after a while.

He's a great professor in one sense - he's very kind and funny and overall a really nice person.  Open to questions etc.  He sings in class and shows us amusing videos  But I don't feel like I learn chemistry from his lectures.  he just demonstrates calculations.  As with Zax, I feel like I learn the most from the supplemental prof Dr. Johnson.

105 was kinda hard.  Only because his freestyle lectures were not supplemented with a textbook, so exams were pretty tricky.  He doesn't TEACH you how to do the problems in class, just shows you what you should know, but with no textbook this is hard to deal with, though I gotta love his singing, family slide shows and random episodes of Buffy.

Love, love, love! Usher is a great professor and an outstanding opera singer. As long as you understand the lectures, textbook chapters, and how lab demonstrates what you're learning in class, you should be fine! Do plenty of practice problems, read the textbook, and go through lecture notes! Not a bad class if you know your stuff...

Amazing Chem teacher who makes use of class time to make sure everyone understands the concepts by going over examples and demonstrations in detail.  He makes class fun and enjoyable with his personal anecdotes and songs.  Overall, great teacher and is a class everyone should take before graduating Cornell.

analysis.name: Anke van Zuylen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: anke is a fantastic cs2800 professor, definitely take it with her. lectures were extremely well prepared and interesting. the hw and prelims are definitely challenging, but they help you understand material at a deeper level and are still fairly manageable.

The class is very well organized, with plenty of help available when needed. Office hours are especially useful for clarifying material. The professor lectures clearly, though the course can be challenging without prior experience in proofs. Homework and exams are demanding but help reinforce key concepts and prepare you well.

ANKE MY GOAT!! Discrete math isn't easy but her lectures were very good at explaining difficult concepts -- I even ended up enjoying the subject! Tests are very fair/doable and we got a lot of good resources to prepare. The hw she writes also significantly improved my understanding. If you must take CS2800 take it with Anke.

The lectures are good, but the textbook is infinitely better (note: there's a slight difference between the lectures and the textbook with NFAs and Thompson's construction, because the textbook doesn't allow NFAs to have lambda transitions but the lectures do). The grading is also very pedantic.

Yes the class it hard but Anke makes it bearable. Nobody wants to take 2800 but she genuinely cares for her students and made the lectures very informative. I will say that the final was very hard. Honestly, if I had to take the class again, I would take it with Anke.

analysis.name: Virginia Doellgast
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I'm not completely sure how this professor is at Cornell. I've ever met a less engaging and more annoying person in my life. I mean truly the worst experience of my academic career. I look at the clock and it doesn't move in this class. I've learned nothing. Made a course that should be interesting so incredibly boring.

ICL is an easy class, no idea why the grade is this low. Her section is about Europe (particularly France/Germany), which is probably the section you'll go into with the most understanding. questions 1-5 on the final are worth 10% each. DO THE LAST QUESTION FIRST its worth 50% and takes a lotta time. Lectures were interesting readings were helpful
The least engaging course I've ever sat through its a complete an utter waste of time and money. They cannot lecture whatsoever. My friend is sleeping as I write this

Writing this before I get my grade back on the first exam. She assigns ~50 pgs of reading/week and isn't the best lecturer. IMO she could improve on linking examples in class to key concepts needed for exams. (this is coming from someone with a >4.0 GPA). This class would also REALLY benefit from having a discussion section (idk how it doesn't)

The worst lecturer of the four professors in this class. Painfully boring, disengaging lectures, and a droning voice. I'm unsure how she became a professor because she has zero public speaking skills. The content is all over the place and doesn't follow a logical sequence. I managed to get an "A" by cramming the study guide.

analysis.name: Kora Battig Von Wittelsbach
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 99 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professoressa is the greatest teacher ever. She is the type of teacher who is there to grow your mind and help you think, and is not just to show off how brilliant she is (even though she is ridiculously brilliant). Definitely take her classes. Her lectures are interesting, inspiring, and engaging. She is also incredibly sweet and an great dresser.

Brilliant professor. The way she incorporated her knowledge of Mediterranean literature, history, culture and her personal experiences of the Mediterranean made every seminar extremely interesting! She is very sweet and approachable, cares a lot about her students and is very passionate about what she is teaching. I truly learned so much from her.

Writing assignments were primarily completed in class, which means the only real homework was reading! This was my favorite class ever. We read engaging books, watched an awe-inspiring documentary, and had our emotions wrenched by two spectacular films. I loved this class because I feel more knowledgeable and more intellectual for having taken it.

Professoressa KVW was my favorite professor I have had at Cornell so far. She is so elegant, intelligent, caring, and has an incredible sense of humor. There was a lot of daily homework that was went over each class (she cold calls), but it is not too hard to complete and is helpful. She genuinely cares a lot about her students and the class a lot.

Professoressa is such a delight to learn from. Having had her for 2 semesters now, I can confidently say that she is one of the most understanding professors here at Cornell! If you are going to learn Italian I HIGHLY, HIGHLY, HIGHLY recommend it be with her. You will thank yourself for picking one of the best Cornell professors around!

analysis.name: Lyrae Van Clief-Stefanon
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Lyrae is a walking inspiration. I took Advanced Poetry with her, and I can honestly say that she completely changed my perspective on writing, and transformed me from a dabbler to a poet. She has given me confidence. She is also sassy and hilarious. Take advantage of this opportunity to take a class with such an amazing human being.

Lyrae is art incarnate. She's inspirational and brings out the artist in everyone. If you don't have semi-thick skin expect some exfoliation, especially if you have little exposure to creative writing. But if you stick through it I promise she'll make you a better writer and a better human. I love her  and you'll love her. Go for it.

The grade for this class is primarily based on attendance. Each class, you have to submit printed copies of either three poems or three pages of prose. There is no feedback on these submissions unless you bring them to workshop classes. Prof. was often very serious during class, which made her feel unapproachable and discouraged participation.

Lyrae is one of the most incredible teachers, and inspiring people I have ever met. I came into her class hating poetry and left after every class understanding something knew about poetry, literature, and life. She cares about her students, engages the class and facilitates great discussions. Take her class. She's amazing.

S:106. Avoid if possible. More than half of the initial class dropped w/in the first week. Harsh and biased grader. Quite mean when things don't go her way. Has stated that she does not like the KIfW. Assigns "group essays". Course books are perverse &/or boring. Deceptively changed the course title. Does not use internet. Great poet, bad teacher.

analysis.name: Vivian Zayas
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: There is a ton of reading, the exams are very hard. I find this class harder than organic chemistry, which is the worst class I've ever taken. The means are low, and there is no curve. Professor Zayas is a very good lecturer, though. Most of the information from lectures is not on the slides, so it is important to go to lecture.

Zayas is a really great lecturer. I found class very engaging and interesting. However, there is a LOT of content to cover in this class. Lecture can move very fast and cover a wide breadth of content. Prelims also cover a ton of content and can be hard if not prepared. Extra credit is nice though.

The slides genuinely do not help you when you are reviewing and you have to come to every class to understand. She doesn't teach you all the content you need to know. The multiple-choice questions are sometimes confusing. The content is pretty interesting though.

Professor Zayas is a great lecturer and made class engaging! The key concepts she provides for each unit were super helpful for studying for exams. The grading system is clear and unbiased, and I found attending lecture to be incredibly useful.

This professor knows her stuff!  And though she may not be the easiest or clearest lecturer, she is able to clear up any confusion if you go to her after class or during office hours.  She is a new prof, so she is still getting the hang of teaching.  Interesting material, difficult tests.  But she practically hands you points (i-Clicker, essays).
